name: Programmer Nano
rulesText: When Programmer Nano enters play, look at the top 3 cards of your deck. Put one into your hand and the rest on the bottom in any order.
flavorText: "He debugs by glaring at the screen until the bug becomes ashamed."
details: Programmer Nano gives you card advantage on entry, letting you dig for the piece you need most.

name: Couch Nap Nano
rulesText: When Couch Nap Nano is attacked, the attacking Character loses 1 Attack until end of turn. If Couch Nap Nano survives the attack, you gain 1 Treat. If you control 2 or more Naps, all Nanos get +1 Defense.
flavorText: "You woke him. That was your first mistake."
details: Couch Nap Nano is a defensive anchor — punishes attackers, rewards survival, and amplifies your whole board if you build around the Nap synergy.

name: Bossman Nano
rulesText: Other Character cards you control get +1 attack. At the start of your turn, gain 1 Treat.
flavorText: "No one knows who put him in charge. Everyone obeys anyway."
details: Bossman Nano strengthens your entire board and steadily increases your resources over time.

name: Deep Sleep Nano
rulesText: When Deep Sleep Nano is attacked, the attacking Character skips its next attack. If Deep Sleep Nano survives the attack, draw a card. If you control 3 or more Naps, your opponent's Characters enter play exhausted.
flavorText: "He's not waking up. You, however, might regret this."
details: Deep Sleep Nano is a fortress — massive defense, attack denial, card draw on survival, and a board-locking passive when paired with enough Nap cards.

name: Aloha Watch Nano
rulesText: At the end of your turn, if you did not play a Character, gain 1 Treat. Whenever a Character enters play, Aloha Watch Nano gets +1 Attack until end of turn.
flavorText: "He's not in a hurry. The sun, the breeze, and the moment will come to him."
details: Aloha Watch Nano rewards patient play and scales in combat every time the board gets more crowded.

name: Death Star Nano
rulesText: When Death Star Nano attacks, deal 1 damage to all enemy Characters. Pay 3 Treats: destroy target Character. This ability may only be used once per turn.
flavorText: "It's not fully operational. It's just resting."
details: Death Star Nano is a late-game finisher — board wipe threat and targeted removal in one terrifying package.

name: Windbreaker Nano
rulesText: When Windbreaker Nano enters play, look at your opponent's hand. Your Characters have First Sniff — they attack before your opponent's Characters each combat.
flavorText: "He saw it coming. He always sees it coming."
details: Windbreaker Nano gives your team initiative and peeks at your opponent's plans the moment he hits the board.

name: Winter Nano
rulesText: Winter Nano cannot be destroyed by a Character with 2 or less Attack. Whenever Winter Nano is attacked, the attacking Character is Frozen — it skips its next attack.
flavorText: "Three sweaters deep. Absolutely nothing is getting through."
details: Winter Nano is an impenetrable wall — laughs at weak attackers and freezes anything strong enough to try.

name: Pretzel Nano
rulesText: Pretzel Nano cannot be chosen as an attack target while you control another Character. When Pretzel Nano would be destroyed, you may pay 1 Treat to return it to your hand instead.
flavorText: "He's fine. Probably."
details: Pretzel Nano is a slippery, hard-to-kill nuisance — hides behind your board and bounces back from destruction.

name: Sith Nano
rulesText: When Sith Nano enters play, take control of target Character with 2 or less Attack until end of turn. If you also control Death Star Nano, the effect is permanent.
flavorText: "The Yoda put up a fight. It did not go well for the Yoda."
details: Sith Nano steals your opponent's small threats and becomes terrifying when paired with Death Star Nano.

name: Sneaker Nap Nano
rulesText: Sneaker Nap Nano counts as 2 Naps for all synergy effects. When Sneaker Nap Nano enters play, all your Nap Characters gain +1 Defense until end of turn.
flavorText: "He dresses for the nap he wants, not the nap he has."
details: Sneaker Nap Nano is a Nap-synergy multiplier — drop him and watch your whole sleeping board get tankier.

name: Hipster Nano
rulesText: Hipster Nano gets +1 Attack for each differently-named Character you control. When Hipster Nano enters play, draw a card if you have no duplicate Characters in play.
flavorText: "He was into Treats before they were mainstream."
details: Hipster Nano rewards a diverse roster — the more unique Characters you run, the harder he hits.

name: Builder Nano
rulesText: At the start of each of your turns, create a 1/1 Cardboard Token. Cardboard Tokens may be sacrificed to pay 1 Treat cost.
flavorText: "He didn't build the furniture. He built an army."
details: Builder Nano is a slow-burn engine — every turn he's alive, your Treat pool grows and your options expand.

name: Taco Nano
rulesText: When Taco Nano attacks and deals damage, gain 1 Treat. If Taco Nano destroys a Character, gain 2 Treats instead.
flavorText: "He wasn't offered any. He didn't let that stop him."
details: Taco Nano is an aggressive resource engine — the more he fights, the more fuel you have to keep going.

name: Camouflage Nano
rulesText: Camouflage Nano cannot be chosen as an attack target until your opponent plays a card. Pay 3 Treats: replace Camouflage Nano with Surprise Attack Nano from your hand.
flavorText: "He was there the whole time. You just weren't looking."
details: Camouflage Nano is a stealthy setup card — protects itself while quietly building toward the Surprise Attack combo.

name: Snow Nano
rulesText: Snow Nano cannot be Frozen. Once per turn, Snow Nano may intercept any attack targeting another Character you control. At the start of your turn, look at the top card of your opponent's deck.
flavorText: "He doesn't mind the cold. The cold minds him."
details: Snow Nano is a resilient protector and intelligence gatherer — immune to freeze, defends your team, and slowly reads your opponent's plans.

name: Surprise Attack Nano
rulesText: If Camouflage Nano is in play when this card enters, Surprise Attack Nano may attack immediately. When Surprise Attack Nano attacks a Character that has not yet attacked this turn, it gets +2 Attack for that combat.
flavorText: "What? There's a dog under those leaves?!?!"
details: Surprise Attack Nano is a devastating ambush finisher — drop him out of Camouflage Nano for an instant, supercharged attack your opponent never saw coming.

name: TA Nano
rulesText: When TA Nano enters play, look at the top 4 cards of your deck — draw 2 and place the rest on the bottom in any order. Once per turn, choose one: Extra Credit — target Character gets +1 Attack and +1 Defense until end of turn; or Failing Grade — target opponent's Character gets -1 Attack and -1 Defense until end of turn.
flavorText: "He has opinions about your formatting."
details: TA Nano is a card-selection engine with a flexible buff or debuff — dig to your best pieces while keeping the board tilted in your favor.

name: Window Seat Nano
rulesText: Window Seat Nano cannot be attacked by Characters that entered play this turn. Once per turn, you may look at your opponent's full hand. If you control 2 or more Characters, Window Seat Nano may attack any Character on the board regardless of position.
flavorText: "He got the window seat. He always gets the window seat."
details: Window Seat Nano is an untouchable intelligence card — safe from new threats, reads your opponent's hand, and strikes anywhere when you build around him.

name: Skydeck Nano
rulesText: Skydeck Nano cannot be targeted by effects or attacks from Characters with 2 or less Attack. At sunset (the start of your third turn with Skydeck Nano in play), he gains +2 Attack permanently.
flavorText: "The higher the stakes, the calmer he gets."
details: Skydeck Nano is untouchable by small threats and becomes a powerhouse the longer he survives — patience pays off.

name: Paris Nano
rulesText: When Paris Nano enters play, your opponent skips their next attack phase — they stop to admire him. At the start of each of your turns while Paris Nano is in play, draw 1 card.
flavorText: "He wore the beret unironically. It worked."
details: Paris Nano is a disruptive presence — shuts down your opponent's attack phase on entry and keeps you up a card every turn he stays on the board.

name: Rainy Day Nano
rulesText: Rainy Day Nano cannot be Frozen or exhausted. Whenever an opponent's effect would reduce your Characters' stats, prevent that reduction. At the start of your turn, if Rainy Day Nano is in play, all your Characters gain +1 Defense until end of turn.
flavorText: "Fully waterproofed. Fully unbothered."
details: Rainy Day Nano is a resilient defensive anchor — immune to status effects, blocks stat reductions, and buffs your whole board every turn just by staying on the field.

name: Magical Dog Crate
rulesText: Play during your opponent's attack phase. Target attacking Character is placed in the Crate — it cannot attack or be attacked for 2 turns. When it returns, it enters exhausted.
details: A well-timed Crate can neutralize your opponent's biggest threat and buy you two full turns to swing the board in your favor.

name: Veterinary Visit
rulesText: Target Character regains its full base Defense and removes all status effects (Frozen, exhausted, stat reductions). If that Character's Defense was reduced this turn, gain 1 Treat.
details: Veterinary Visit keeps your best Characters in the fight and can recover a Treat when played reactively.

name: The Zoomies
rulesText: Target Character may attack twice this turn and ignores First Sniff effects. After the second attack, that Character is exhausted.
details: The Zoomies turns any Character into a double-strike threat — best saved for a killing blow or to punch through two blockers at once.

name: Cone of Shame
rulesText: Target opponent's Character gets -2 Attack and cannot use activated abilities until end of turn. If its Attack becomes 0, it also cannot block.
details: Cone of Shame neuters your opponent's key threat for a full turn — especially brutal against ability-heavy Characters like Death Star Nano or Sith Nano.

name: Squeaky Toy
rulesText: Place a Squeaky Toy token (0/1) on your side of the field. All opponent's Characters must attack the Squeaky Toy this turn instead of your Characters or players. When the Squeaky Toy is destroyed, draw a card.
details: Squeaky Toy shields your entire board for a turn and replaces itself on destruction — great for stalling while you set up a big play.

name: Bath Time
rulesText: All opponent's Characters get -1 Attack and -1 Defense until end of turn. Characters that reach 0 Defense are destroyed.
details: Bath Time is a cheap board sweeper that punishes low-Defense boards and combos brutally with Cone of Shame.

name: Bone Treat
rulesText: Add 1 Treat to your pool.

name: Chew Toy Treat
rulesText: Add 2 Treats to your pool.

name: Salmon Treat
rulesText: Add 3 Treats to your pool.

name: Bully Stick Treat
rulesText: Add 5 Treats to your pool.
